title: asplenium subglandulosum
intro: Asplenium subglandulosum, also known as the maidenhair spleenwort, is a type of fern that belongs to the Aspleniaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits of Asplenium subglandulosum include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an antioxidant, and provide relief from coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
